audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for the park-wake-protocol-hole class (runloom bugs 7,14,15,16,17,19,23,33,36,37,49,64,77,80,81,85,96,97,116). Every blocking primitive must satisfy ALL of: (1) PARK-UNTIL-CONDITION: the caller re-checks its predicate in a while(!pred) loop and RE-REGISTERS as a waiter each iteration -- flag any code that parks once on a single wake signal and returns/consumes without re-checking (bugs 80,96,97). (2) ATOMIC COMMIT: an ARMED->PARKED->WOKEN commit FSM (or equivalent single-CAS) closes the window between last predicate recheck and the actual yield so a waker cannot claim the parker in-between (bugs 14,19). (3) COMPLETE WAKE-ROUTE FANOUT: a wake must hit EVERY route the target could be blocked on for this hub -- idle_cond AND ring eventfd AND pump AND (Windows) IOCP post; list the wake routes this primitive can block on and confirm the waker touches all of them (bugs 15,23,85). (4) OWNER ROUTING: the woken g is enqueued on ITS OWN scheduler/hub, never the waker's (bug 77). (5) SINGLE CLAIMER: exactly one waker consumes one parker via CAS; two wakers (close-hook + auto-completion, spurious + real) cannot both proceed (bugs 56,64,81). (6) CORRECT LOCK/QUEUE: the lock taken to unpark is derived from the parker's committed hub, not a lock-free racing p->hub read (bug 64). For each violation, name which of the 6 holds it and give the exact interleaving that loses or double-serves the wake. Cross-check against docs/dev/wake_protocol/README.md and the negative controls in tools/verify/spin (netpoll_commit.pml BUG_NO_COMMIT, wake_state.pml, cross_thread_wake.pml BUG_ROUTE_TO_WAKER, park_generic_timed.pml).
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(runloom_(mn|coro)_park|wait_fd|iouring_do|park_waiter|_Parker|CoEvent|CoSemaphore|CoCondition|chan_(send|recv)|select)\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom/monkey src/runloom/aio | rg -v 'while\s*\(' ; then for each hit inspect for a missing while(!pred) re-register loop. Structural surfacer for single-wake-trust: rg -nP 'park[^;]*;\s*(?!.*while)' and rg -nl 'idle_cond' src/runloom_c | xargs rg -L 'ring.*eventfd|pump|iocp' (wake sites touching only one route).
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/*park*, src/runloom_c/netpoll_*.c.inc, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_*.c.inc, src/runloom_c/chan_*.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_*.c.inc, src/runloom/monkey/{_base,events,queues}.py, src/runloom/aio/_base.py; ANY new function named *park*/*wake*/*unpark*; ANY new blocking primitive or new wake-route added to a hub.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) enumerate every park entry with `rg -noP 'runloom_[a-z_]*park[a-z_]*'` and every wake site with `rg -noP 'runloom_[a-z_]*wake[a-z_]*'` across src/runloom_c + monkey + aio; (b) for the specific defect sub-shape (single-wake-trust / missing-commit / incomplete-route / waker-routing / double-claim / re-park-no-reregister) grep every OTHER primitive for the SAME sub-shape -- e.g. if a re-park-without-re-register bug is found in CoEvent, check CoSimpleQueue, chan select, wait_fd, iouring_do; (c) add/point the matching Spin/GenMC negative control (BUG_SINGLE_PARK_TRUST, BUG_MISSING_ROUTE, BUG_ROUTE_TO_WAKER, BUG_DOUBLE_CLAIM, BUG_REPARK_NO_REREGISTER) at each sibling before closing.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for the api-contract-misread class (runloom bugs 13,18,54,59,68,82,86,89,102,107,109,117,118,119,125). For EVERY external-API edge behavior the code depends on, the code must match the SPEC or a reference implementation (Linux man pages / io_uring liburing / kqueue Go+libuv+mio / wepoll+AFD / stock CPython asyncio+socket+threading / fiber ABI). Flag any place the behavior was ASSUMED. Specifically check: EPOLLET+EPOLLEXCLUSIVE delivery (edge fires once, must re-register; poll-on-epoll-fd bridge visibility) (bugs 18,86); kqueue EV_EOF/EV_ERROR live in flags not filter, and EV_ONESHOT collapses so first-match strands siblings, plus EV_CLOEXEC (bug 59); AFD bit-direction folding (READ vs WRITE teardown bits are distinct, LOCAL_CLOSE, socket-only handles) (bugs 54,125); io_uring CQ-overflow/NODROP backlog (kernel stops re-signalling eventfd) and offset default must be -1 not 0 for use-and-advance files (bugs 82,89); ABI rounding-mode regs FPCR/MXCSR saved across switch (bug 13); asyncio coro.send(None) not send(result), call_soon FIFO ordering, done-callback deferral order (bugs 117,118,119); socket.settimeout/setblocking layering and buffered input() below the fd (bugs 107,109); BoundedSemaphore permit accounting excludes queued waiters (bug 102); Ctrl-C/signal servicing in the run loop (bug 68). For each dependency print: the exact fold assumed, the reference source that contradicts it, and the failure mode. Do NOT answer edge-behavior questions from memory -- cite the reference file/line.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(EPOLLET|EPOLLEXCLUSIVE|EV_ONESHOT|EV_EOF|EV_ERROR|EV_CLOEXEC|AFD_POLL|IORING_[A-Z_]+|FPCR|MXCSR|FXSAVE|call_soon|send\(None\)|settimeout|setblocking|BoundedSemaphore)\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom ; and cross-check each hit file has a sibling contracts/ or *_sem/ memo -- rg -L 'contract|reference|man |liburing|libuv|CPython' on the hit file flags an unsourced use.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/netpoll_iocp.c, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_*.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_register.c.inc, src/runloom_c/arch/swap_*.S, src/runloom_c/fcontext.c, src/runloom/aio/*.py, src/runloom/monkey/{sockets,events,polling}.py; docs/dev/KQUEUE_AUDIT_2026-06.md; ANY diff introducing a new EPOLL*/EV_*/AFD_*/IORING_* identifier or a new asyncio-protocol callback/send site.
variant_analysis: Given one misread: (a) grep every use of that identifier/API across the whole tree (`rg -n 'EV_ONESHOT'`) and re-audit each against the reference fold; (b) the four platform poll backends (epoll/kqueue/AFD-IOCP/io_uring) are semantic mirrors -- a misread in one implies checking the analogous fold in the other three (EOF-in-flags -> check AFD teardown bits -> check io_uring res encoding); (c) for asyncio folds, diff runloom behavior against STOCK CPython via the differential conformance harness (tests item 8, GO_DIFFERENTIAL.md); (d) record the fold + citation in a per-API semantics memo so the next author cannot re-assume it.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for cross-thread-tls-affinity (runloom bugs 9,11,27,28,29,30,31,32,72,73,111). Free-threaded CPython and mimalloc bind mutable state to exactly ONE os thread/tstate; M:N multiplexing N fibers over one hub tstate breaks the bind one field at a time. Enumerate EVERY per-tstate / per-thread-bound piece of state a fiber can mutate and confirm it is EITHER snapshotted+restored per fiber (in runloom_sched_pystate.c.inc snap/load) OR proven owner-only. Check at minimum: tstate->exc_info / current_exception chain (bug 9); c_stack_refs and c_stack_top/soft/hard recursion fields new in 3.14t (bugs 11,12); contextvars Context + c_profilefunc/c_tracefunc hooks (bug 72); decimal ts->context / thread-local localcontext (bug 73); gilstate TSS binding and delete_later trashcan chain (bugs 29,74); the tstate pointer cached inside a SUSPENDED eval frame -- a fiber that yielded mid-eval carries its origin hub's tstate baked into the C frame, so it can NEVER be resumed on a different hub (bugs 31,111). Two whole designs (per-g-tstate with Python handlers; cross-hub snap migration) are STRUCTURALLY unsound, not fixable -- flag any design that attaches a second thread to one tstate (bug 27), reads instantaneous fiber-detach state to migrate (bug 28), or moves a live-framed fiber across hubs (bugs 30,31,32). Output per field: snapshotted / owner-only-proven / UNGUARDED, and for any migration design state whether it violates the one-tstate-per-thread STW protocol or the mimalloc owner-page contract.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '->(exc_info|current_exception|exc_state|context\b|c_stack_[a-z_]+|c_recursion_remaining|py_recursion_remaining|c_profilefunc|c_tracefunc|delete_later|datastack_[a-z]+)' src/runloom_c ; plus rg -nP '_PyThreadState_(Attach|Detach|Bind|Swap)|PyGILState|tstate' src/runloom_c/mn_sched_*.c.inc src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_pystate.c.inc -- every tstate-field deref that is NOT inside the snap/load functions is a candidate.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_pystate.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_handoff.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_hub_resume_preempt.c.inc, src/runloom_c/coro.c, src/runloom_c/runloom_iframe.c, src/runloom/monkey/polling.py; ANY CPython version bump (new tstate fields), ANY new _PyThreadState_Attach/Detach call, ANY code that resumes a g on a different hub than it parked on.
variant_analysis: Given one leaked field: (a) diff the FULL PyThreadState / _PyThreadStateImpl struct for the target CPython version against the snap/load field list in runloom_sched_pystate.c.inc -- every struct field with a written disposition (snap-own/snap-borrow/owner-only/forbidden) or the audit is incomplete; (b) grep every fiber-entry and fiber-resume path for reads of that field class; (c) if the instance is a migration/attach design, do NOT patch -- prove it against the one-tstate-per-thread STW protocol and mimalloc owner-page contract (LIFECYCLE_INVARIANTS.md #2) and abandon it if unsound; (d) add sizeof/offsetof _Static_asserts per version so a new-field omission becomes a build break.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for shutdown-fork-teardown (runloom bugs 24,26,41,42,44,60,62,63,69,70,83). Teardown is a PROTOCOL, not a destructor list. For fini/close/fork/hub-exit/loop-close/SIGINT, verify: (1) every parked waiter is WOKEN-WITH-ERROR or cancelled -- epoll/kqueue emit NO event for a closed fd and io_uring PINS the struct file, so the wake is the runtime's job, not the kernel's (bugs 60,62,63,83); a fiber parked on accept/recv when close() fires from another fiber must be actively woken. (2) fork: register_at_fork resets the WHOLE M:N runtime in the child -- dead hubs, pending counters, netpoll/epoll fd, and every lock the child inherits LOCKED by a dead owner is re-init'd (bugs 41,42); the fd_armed cache is invalidated because the child's epoll registration did not survive (bug 43, see stale-cache lens). (3) TLS caches (chunk/coro/stack pools) are flushed at hub exit, not abandoned (bug 44). (4) joins are ordered against stop-the-world -- never pthread_join while the joiner is ATTACHED and a starting thread wants STW (bug 26). (5) post-teardown stale handles are rejected by a SESSION GENERATION counter (runloom_mn_gen), not a NULL check -- a foreign g.wake() after mn_fini is a generation-rejected no-op (bug 69). (6) function-pointer hooks (eval_frame) swapped at fini do not leave a foreign thread indirect-calling through NULL (bug 24). (7) a daemon's running flag is published AFTER its handle so stop() never joins a stale handle (bug 70). For each blocking resource in SUBSYSTEM, output its teardown story for EACH of the 6 vectors, or mark the (resource,vector) cell UNCOVERED.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(mn_fini|register_at_fork|pthread_atfork|_hub_exit|loop\.close|cancel_fd|cancel_all_parked|close\()\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom ; and to find close paths missing a wake: rg -nl 'close\(' src/runloom_c | xargs rg -L 'cancel|unpark|wake|drain_parked' (a TU that closes fds but never wakes parkers is a candidate). Global-mutable-not-atfork-classified: rg -nP 'static\s+(_Atomic\s+)?(long|int|pthread_mutex_t|.*_pool_t)\s+runloom_' src/runloom_c cross-referenced against the atfork reset list.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/mn_sched_init_fini.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_init.c.inc, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_msclose.c.inc, src/runloom/{__init__,context}.py, src/runloom/monkey/{_base,executors,locks}.py, src/runloom/aio/*.py; docs/dev/mn_fini_hang.md; ANY new blocking resource, new process-global mutable, new lock, or new cached fd; ANY new close/cancel entry point.
variant_analysis: Given one teardown gap: (a) build the park-site x teardown-vector matrix -- enumerate every park site (`rg -noP 'runloom_[a-z_]*park[a-z_]*'`) and every global mutable, cross every one against {mn_fini, fork-child, fd-close, hub-exit, loop-close, post-fini-foreign-call}; the found bug is one cell, so audit every other cell in the same row (this resource under the other 5 vectors) and column (the other resources under this vector); (b) for fork specifically, classify every process-global as at-fork-reset / const-safe / generation-guarded; (c) re-run the RunloomMnFini.tla quiescence model with the new route; (d) verify the completeness check that diffs the park-site list against the teardown manifest so the next added resource cannot merge uncovered.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for error-cancel-path-untested (runloom bugs 46,52,57,58,61,66,88,100,101,108,120,121,122). Failure/cancellation arms almost never run in CI, so their bugs ship. Force-read every error and cancel arm and check: (1) POSITIVE-SENTINEL vs result<0: RUNLOOM_NETPOLL_CANCELLED (0x40000000, netpoll.h:33) and RUNLOOM_NETPOLL_SIGNALED (0x20000000) are POSITIVE, so any `if (result < 0)` or `result < 0 == error` idiom SILENTLY MISSES them -- this recurred 4x (module_tcp, backstop, compiled handlers, osio/pidfd) (bug 61); flag every wait_fd/iouring return consumer that checks sign instead of the sentinel bits. (2) cancel_all/cancel_fd must leave the woken waiter's PREDICATE consistent -- waking a waiter with predicate still False re-parks it forever (bug 101); and must not leave state un-rolled-back. (3) submit/spawn failure must roll back: failed io_uring_enter must roll back sq_tail or the stale SQE ships and shifts completions off-by-one (bug 88); failed driver-fiber spawn must roll back the half-registered task or loop.close gathers forever (bug 122); dropped go() stack_size arg must not silently TypeError in the root fiber (bug 108). (4) DEL-ok+ADD-fail migration must error-wake pre-existing parkers left in NO epoll (bug 52). (5) early close before pool-mutex init must not lock a zeroed CRITICAL_SECTION (bug 57); BSS-zero fd slots must not match fd 0 (bug 58). (6) re-entrant non-blocking RLock acquire must count++ (bug 100). (7) a DEBUG tripwire must not reintroduce the very hang production healed (bug 46). (8) fd_read/write must set O_NONBLOCK and validate the fd range (bug 66). (9) cancelled timer/task runner closures must not capture the callback graph (bug 120). Output each error/cancel arm and whether it is exercised by a fault-injection test.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(res|ret|result|n)\s*<\s*0' src/runloom_c src/runloom | rg -iP 'wait_fd|netpoll|iouring|poll' -- candidate positive-sentinel-slip sites; plus rg -nP 'CANCELLED|SIGNALED' src/runloom_c to list every sentinel producer, then verify each consumer masks the bit rather than sign-testing. Cancel arms: rg -nP 'cancel_(all|fd|g)|rollback|sq_tail|_at_exit|register_at' src/runloom_c src/runloom.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/netpoll_wait_fd.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_register.c.inc, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_*.c.inc, src/runloom_c/module_tcp.c.inc, src/runloom/monkey/events.py, src/runloom/aio/*.py; ANY new consumer of a wait_fd/iouring/netpoll return; ANY new cancel_/rollback/error arm; ANY new submit-then-fail path.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) for the positive-sentinel sub-shape, grep EVERY consumer of a sentinel-returning function tree-wide and convert sign tests to bit-mask tests -- the recurrence count (4x) proves siblings exist; (b) for a cancel-leaves-predicate-unset bug, sweep every cancel_all/cancel_fd/timeout arm across chan, netpoll, io_uring, and every monkey primitive; (c) drive the arm via the counted fault-injection sweep (tools/fault_sweep_counted.py + the netpoll_init.c.inc RUNLOOM_FAULT_* injection sites) at EVERY park site x each failure {EBUSY/EAGAIN/EINTR, DEL-ok/ADD-fail, spawn-alloc-fail, early-close}; (d) add a poisoned-high-bits sentinel oracle so any future result<0 use of a positive sentinel traps at first sweep.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for object-reuse-aba (runloom bugs 1,2,3,4,5,6,21,40,55,65,75,79,110). Zero-alloc designs place live records on RECYCLABLE substrate (coro stacks, g slabs, datastack chunk pools, chan waiter records, parkers, IOCP/IRP ctxs, io_uring handles, fd numbers, cached fileno). Any stale in-flight reference (dup wake, still-linked waiter, lingering IRP, cached fileno) that meets a byte-identical reuse is a UAF/wrong-target. For every recyclable record and every stored reference to it, verify: (1) VALIDATED RESOLUTION: cross-thread-visible references are generation-stamped handles resolved via try_incref-before-CAS that returns NULL on stale, NOT raw pointers -- a freed runloom_g never returns to the OS (slab_free retains, magic DEAD) precisely so a stale dup-wake's try_incref reads a valid refcount (bugs 2,3); (2) UNLINK-BEFORE-REUSE: a woken/spurious waiter is confirmed UNLINKED from its queue before its stack/slab is reused -- park-until-actually-unlinked, not a spot check (bugs 1,6,21); (3) FULL SCRUB on recycle -- every field including pass_index/c_arg is cleared or the recycled fiber runs stale args (bug 40); (4) GRACE/quarantine delay before a datastack chunk or handle is reused, because CPython holds raw ->previous/frame pointers into it (bugs 5,75); (5) kernel-side ctxs (AFD IRP, io_uring user_data, pending_wake stash, cached fileno) are refcounted or generation-checked so a close+fd-reuse mid-park does not write onto an unrelated live socket (bugs 55,65,79,110). Flag every raw pointer or cached fileno stored across a possible free, and every recycle path missing a scrub or quarantine.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(slab_free|_pool_(acquire|release|free)|recycle|scrub|quarantine|fileno\(\)|free_list|->previous|user_data|GRACE)\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom ; plus find stored-fileno-across-park: rg -nP 'fileno|->fd\b' src/runloom/monkey/sockets.py src/runloom_c/*.inc | rg -B2 -A5 park. Structural: rg -nl '_pool_release|slab_free' src/runloom_c | xargs rg -L 'gen|magic|scrub|memset|try_incref' (a release path with no generation/scrub is a candidate).
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/mn_sched_mn_api.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_core*.inc, src/runloom_c/chan_waiters.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_parkers.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_iocp.c, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_msclose.c.inc, src/runloom_c/coro.c (datastack/chunk pool), src/runloom/monkey/sockets.py; ANY new pool/slab/free-list, ANY new record placed on a coro stack, ANY cached fileno/user_data/IRP ctx, ANY bulk-free-to-OS path.
variant_analysis: Given one ABA: (a) enumerate EVERY recyclable pool (`rg -n '_pool_|slab_|free_list|chunk'`) and every field annotated as cross-thread-visible; the fix generalizes to a generation-stamped handle -- apply the SAME handle discipline to every sibling pool; (b) for a bulk-free-to-OS regression (bug 3), confirm quarantine/never-free-to-OS is the DEFAULT so a future bulk-arena cannot silently reopen the class; (c) run the pool under a torture mode (0xDD poison + randomized quarantine delay + per-deref generation check) and an mprotect/electric-fence mode in nightly lifefuzz -- glibc/x86 keep freed memory benign, so only Windows page-heap / arm64 / poison surfaces these; (d) point the CBMC handle-CAS harness (sched_qref_cbmc.c) at the new pool's handle type.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for blocking-at-unsafe-point (runloom bugs 22,53,78,94,95,103,105). In M:N the current OS thread IS the scheduler, so ordinary threaded idioms freeze whole hubs or deadlock stop-the-world. Flag EVERY: (1) real OS lock held across code that can PARK, YIELD, or be PREEMPTED -- a preempted goroutine holding an offload-queue or _Parker pool lock freezes every hub waiting on it (bug 94); pool.lock held across Py_END_ALLOW_THREADS creates pool.lock->GIL vs GIL->pool.lock ABBA (bug 53). (2) constructing a _Parker (which itself PARKS, e.g. socketpair() init) INSIDE a guarded critical section (bug 103). (3) staying tstate-ATTACHED or holding idle_lock across an STW-blocking attach point (bug 78). (4) yielding/preempting inside tp_dealloc / a finalizer -- freezes a half-destroyed object across a GC safe-point -> UAF; both yield sites must gate on runloom_tstate_in_destruction and DEFER (bug 22). (5) capturing a RAW pre-patch _thread lock (e.g. tempfile._once_lock) that the wrapped-lock accounting cannot see, then holding it across a yielding probe (bug 105). (6) a single shared SimpleQueue/OS-mutex convoy that parks hub OS threads off the scheduler at scale (bugs 95,104). Rule set to enforce: detach/release-before-blocking; lock-free structures on hub paths; a cannot-park guard (noyield_depth) around dealloc/finalizer/preempt-callback/shim critical sections; the preempt hook must DEFER while any ranked OS lock is held (Go's m->locks). Output every lock-across-blocking, parker-in-guard, and yield-in-destruction site with the freeze scenario.
grep_or_query: clang-query or rg heuristic: rg -nP '(lock|Lock|acquire|EnterCriticalSection)\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom/monkey | then within +/-30 lines rg 'park|yield|ALLOW_THREADS|Attach|socketpair|Py_END|coro_resume'. Concretely: rg -nP 'pool\.lock|idle_lock|_Parker\(|allocate_lock|SimpleQueue' src/runloom_c src/runloom/monkey ; and rg -nP 'Py_(BEGIN|END)_ALLOW_THREADS' src/runloom_c to check no ranked lock is held across the GIL window. lockrank held-stack (runloom_lockrank.h) assert placement is the mechanical check.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/netpoll_pump.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_preempt*.inc, src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_core.c.inc, src/runloom/monkey/{_base,events}.py; ANY new lock acquisition on a hub/pump path, ANY new _Parker construction, ANY tp_dealloc/finalizer/preempt-callback body, ANY new Py_BEGIN/END_ALLOW_THREADS wrapper.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) walk the lockrank held-rank stack (runloom_lockrank.h) -- every ranked OS lock: grep every site that holds it and check none can park/yield/preempt/reattach-GIL while held; (b) for the parker-in-guard shape, grep every guarded critical section for _Parker/ socketpair/ blocking init inside it; (c) for the yield-in-destruction shape, confirm BOTH preempt yield sites gate on runloom_tstate_in_destruction and audit every finalizer/dealloc; (d) place the four choke-point asserts (park/yield entry aborts if ranked lock held or noyield_depth>0; ALLOW_THREADS asserts no ranked lock; join/cond_wait asserts DETACHED; preempt hook defers while lock held) so the next occurrence is a first-run abort, in release builds not just debug.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for mn-identity-liveness (runloom bugs 25,34,35,71,92,99,106,124). 1:1-world identity and liveness assumptions go silently false under M:N; symptoms are STRANDED WORK and FALSE OWNERSHIP, presenting as unexplained hangs, not crashes. Check every assumption of the forms: (1) OS TID == execution identity -- mp recursive SemLock/RLock keyed re-entrancy on OS TID treats distinct fibers on one hub as one owner (bug 99); re-key identity on the FIBER. (2) the calling thread's scheduler == THE scheduler -- time/context timer goroutines spawned via single-thread go() under M:N never fire (bug 124); route _spawn to the real runtime. (3) a hub always drains its own queue -- a blocking C call or GC STW monopoly means a woken/runnable g strands head-of-line (bugs 25,34); liveness accounting must count every parked-but-WAKEABLE entity, and a wedged hub's work must be stealable / handed off. (4) the sweeper is the sole resumer -- once stealing exists, MADV_DONTNEED idle-stack sweep races a stolen resume (bug 35); need a SWEEPING/SWEEPING_WOKEN handshake. (5) instantaneous fiber-state reads are stable -- routing/introspection on a transient DETACHED window misroutes (bugs 28,106) or tears a torn read of a second single-thread scheduler's live g->snap (bug 71); gate on a stable _runtime_live signal, not a snapshot. (6) run() liveness must NOT abandon a fiber chan-parked on a mutex a FOREIGN thread holds -- mark it foreign_wakeable or it is falsely counted dead and the process hangs (bug 92). Output each identity/liveness assumption and whether it survives M:N + stealing + foreign threads.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '\b(gettid|pthread_self|GetCurrentThreadId|os\.getpid|current_g|owner.*tid|_spawn|go\(|runloom_go\b|liveness|pending|runnable|MADV_DONTNEED)\b' src/runloom_c src/runloom ; concretely for identity-keyed-on-TID: rg -nP 'tid|thread_id|threading\.get_ident' src/runloom/monkey ; for wrong-scheduler spawn: rg -nP '\bgo\(|_go_|spawn' src/runloom/{time,context}.py src/runloom/monkey ; for liveness undercount: rg -nP 'pending|live|wakeable|abandon' src/runloom_c/mn_sched_*.inc.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/mn_sched_hub_main.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_sysmon.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_handoff.c.inc, src/runloom/monkey/{executors,_base}.py, src/runloom/{time,context}.py; ANY new use of an OS TID/thread-ident as an identity key, ANY new go()/spawn call site, ANY new liveness/pending counter, ANY new work-stealing or sweep path.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) grep every OS-TID / thread-ident identity key and every go()/spawn site tree-wide; each is a candidate for the same fiber-vs-thread confusion; (b) for a liveness-undercount, audit run()'s accounting against EVERY way a g can be parked-but-wakeable (chan on foreign-held mutex, netpoll, io_uring, timer) -- the deadlock detector currently treats netpoll-parked as wakeable but genuine stranded parks are silent, so extend the parked-entity ledger to cover the found route; (c) drive the fiber-on-hub x {foreign-thread contention, blocking-C-call head-of-line, GC STW, stealing} matrix with a SIGUSR1 fiber census oracle; (d) for transient-state misroute, replace every instantaneous fiber-state read on a routing path with the stable _runtime_live gate.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for stale-cache-vs-kernel (runloom bugs 43,45,47,48,50,51,87). The zero-syscall register fast path caches kernel epoll registration in a PROCESS-GLOBAL fd_armed table keyed by FD NUMBER (netpoll_register.c.inc). Every path that mutates kernel epoll state MUST also invalidate/update the cache, or a later fd-number reuse parks forever far from the cause. Verify EVERY such path: (1) GC dealloc / socketmodule close that closes the raw fd out-of-band bypasses the unregister hook -> stale-arm poison on reuse; the durable fix is a self-validating probe (poll ground-truth before trusting the cache), not another enumerated hook (bug 45). (2) raw close() bypassing the runtime hook (all-C echo, sock_*, pidfd, pipe) leaves a stale LEVEL arm -> release_if_idle on completion (bug 47). (3) fork resets the epoll but not the cache (bug 43, also shutdown lens). (4) scheduler-mode switch / cross-pool migration changes WHICH epoll matters -> fd left armed in an epoll no live hub pumps (bugs 48,87). (5) migration re-arm must compute cur|need, NOT target=need -- narrowing the mask drops events and a poisoned cache makes the loss permanent (bug 50). (6) epoll_ctl issued OUTSIDE pool.lock lets concurrent registers interleave and silently narrow the kernel event set (bug 51). Confirm: mask law (arm may only widen cur|need; narrowing is a distinct drain/release-only op); epoll_ctl + cache mutation both under pool.lock with a lockrank assert; an epoch bumped by fork/mode-switch/migration compared on every fast-path hit. Output every kernel-state-mutating path and whether it invalidates the cache.
grep_or_query: rg -nl 'epoll_ctl' src/runloom_c  (currently spans mn_sched_hub_main, runloom_tcp, io_uring_l_loop, netpoll_diag_fd, netpoll_register, netpoll_pump_helpers, netpoll_init, netpoll_wake_iouring, netpoll_wait_fd -- 9 TUs; the chokepoint goal is ONE). Then rg -nl '\bclose\(' src/runloom_c src/runloom | xargs rg -L 'unregister|release_if_idle|invalidate|fd_armed' (close paths that never touch the arm cache). Mask-narrowing: rg -nP 'fd_armed|arm.*mask|EPOLL_CTL_(ADD|MOD|DEL)' src/runloom_c/netpoll_register.c.inc.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/netpoll_register.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_wait_fd.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_init.c.inc, any TU that calls epoll_ctl or close(2), src/runloom/monkey/sockets.py; ANY new close/unregister path, ANY scheduler-mode-switch or fd-migration code, ANY change to the fd_armed cache or its key, ANY fork handler.
variant_analysis: Given one stale-cache bug: (a) enumerate ALL epoll_ctl and close(2) call sites (`rg -nl 'epoll_ctl|close('`) -- funnel them toward one netpoll_kernel_ops TU and audit each remaining bypass; (b) for each kernel-mutating path, confirm the invalidate->cancel/error-wake-every-parker->close order (close emits no epoll event, so the wake is mandatory); (c) add an always-on differ oracle: whenever a parker is parked > T ms, diff fd_armed against /proc/self/fdinfo/<epfd> and abort with expected-vs-actual mask; (d) run the forced-fd-reuse adversary (LD_PRELOAD dup2-poison every closed fd number) over the bypass paths; (e) re-run the netpoll_arm_demonic CBMC oracle extended with the close_raw/reuse/fork/mode_switch/migrate alphabet against the refinement invariant.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for lifetime-refcount-gap (runloom bugs 4,6,10,39,74,79). A queue/timer/snapshot/waiter stored a RAW or BORROWED pointer assuming an implicit owner keeps it alive ('the fiber's frames pin the generator', 'single-owner convention'); fiber suspension stretches the free race from nanoseconds to arbitrary time. For EVERY cross-thread-visible or suspension-surviving stored pointer, verify it takes its OWN ref (or is generation-validated): (1) timer entries must hold a g refcount -- drain_timers must not CAS on a freed g (bug 4); (2) a completed wait_fd parker must be UNLINKED, not left linked for the next cancel/pump to UAF (bug 6); (3) a snapshotted exc_info that BORROWS a generator's gi_exc_state dangles if the generator dies before resume -- own it (bug 10); (4) refcount the RIGHT thing -- Py_XINCREF'ing tstate->delete_later refcounts through the ob_tid trashcan chain and scrambles a dying object (bug 74, refcounting the wrong field is as fatal as none); (5) an io_uring multishot handle freed by a terminal/close CQE under a parked ms_recv must be refcounted so the parked reader's reference stays valid (bug 79); (6) objects reachable only through channel buffers must be visible to cyclic GC or they leak (bug 39). Output each stored pointer, its assumed owner, whether that owner actually outlives every reader across suspension, and whether the ref is on the CORRECT field.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '(borrow|->snap|gi_exc_state|delete_later|XINCREF|_entry->g|timer.*g\b|ms_handle|ctx->)' src/runloom_c ; and to find stored-raw-pointer-across-park: rg -nP 'struct\s+runloom_g\s*\*|->g\s*=' src/runloom_c | rg -v 'incref|refcount|gen' . GC-visibility: rg -nP 'tp_traverse|tp_clear|Py_VISIT' src/runloom_c/chan.c (a container storing PyObjects without traverse is a leak candidate).
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_parkwake.c.inc, src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_pystate.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_drain.c.inc, src/runloom_c/io_uring_l_msclose.c.inc, src/runloom_c/chan.c; ANY new struct field storing a g*/PyObject*/handle that outlives the storing call, ANY new timer/snapshot/waiter record, ANY new container of Python objects.
variant_analysis: Given one gap: (a) grep every stored g*/PyObject*/kernel-ctx pointer and classify each as owned-ref / generation-validated / proven-outlives-reader; (b) the shipped fix is always 'take a counted ref' or 'generation-stamp' -- apply to every sibling record (timers, parkers, snapshots, IRP ctxs, multishot handles); (c) for the refcount-the-wrong-field trap, verify the ref is on the object whose lifetime you need, not an aliasing chain field (ob_tid/delete_later/->previous); (d) build the ownership model in fanalyzer/CBMC/Spin (the class was found as much by ownership models as by tests) so the suspension-stretched window is proven closed.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for foreign-thread-contract (runloom bugs 38,90,91,123). Monkey-patching the stdlib guarantees PLAIN OS THREADS (mp.Queue _feed feeders, ThreadPool/executor workers, pre-run callers before the loop starts) reach fiber-only primitives. Uncontended/fast paths work everywhere, so isolation tests pass; only the CONTENDED or PARKING path crashes or spins. For EVERY public/monkey-patched primitive (chan send/recv/select, Mutex.lock/_acquire, CoEvent/CoSemaphore/CoCondition, CoSimpleQueue, mp.Queue, executors, call_soon/create_task) verify: (1) a NON-ALLOCATING 'am I a fiber' peek at entry -- runloom_tls_sched / peek_current, NEVER runloom_sched_get() which MALLOCS on a foreign waker and was itself a recurring sub-bug (bugs 90,91); (2) a real OS-blocking fallback (or a loud clean error) when the caller is a foreign thread -- blocking chan/select with no goroutine must RAISE, not busy-spin forever (bug 38); a contended Mutex.lock from a foreign thread must block on the OS, not raise 'channel recv would block' (bug 91, the uncontended CAS path masked it -- only 8x suite load exposed it); (3) pre-run foreign-thread call_soon/create_task must land on the runtime scheduler that WILL be drained, not the caller's never-drained scheduler (bug 123). Output every primitive entry point, whether it does the non-allocating peek, and what its foreign-thread path does under CONTENTION.
grep_or_query: rg -nP 'sched_get\(\)' src/runloom_c src/runloom  -- every hit reachable from a public primitive is a candidate (must be peek_current/tls_sched instead). Then rg -nP 'peek_current|runloom_tls_sched|current_g|_is_fiber|no.?goroutine' src/runloom/{sync.py,monkey} to confirm each entry peeks. Entry-point census: rg -nP 'def (send|recv|select|acquire|_acquire|lock|release|wait|set|put|get|call_soon|create_task)\b' src/runloom/{sync.py,monkey,aio}.
trigger_paths: src/runloom/sync.py (36 call sites), src/runloom/monkey/{_base,events,queues,executors,locks}.py, src/runloom/aio/_base.py, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_mn_api.c.inc; ANY new public/monkey-patched primitive, ANY new entry that could be reached from mp feeder / executor / pre-run threads, ANY use of sched_get near an entry point.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) grep every runloom_sched_get() call and confirm none sits on a primitive-entry check path (replace with the non-allocating peek); (b) enumerate every public primitive and drive the FULL matrix {caller: fiber-on-hub, raw threading.Thread, mp feeder thread, pre-run thread} x {uncontended, contended-by-fiber, contended-by-thread} with contention FORCED (a fiber holds the resource across a park so foreign threads always take the slow path); the found bug is one cell, so every primitive's contended-foreign cell needs the same audit; (c) oracle: hang_hunter deadline per cell + exactly-once accounting + a debug assert that fires if the allocating sched_get is reached thread-foreign; (d) run under TSan-FT and pydebug lanes.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for ft-check-then-act (runloom bugs 76,93,98,115). GIL-era 'if flag: mutate' and plain-field handshakes are NON-ATOMIC on free-threaded CPython -- they passed single-threaded tests because the GIL made them atomic by accident. Flag EVERY check-then-act on shared state: (1) 'if deque: popleft' / 'if not empty: take' TOCTOU -- two fibers/threads both pass the emptiness check and double-pop or IndexError (bug 98, CoSimpleQueue); (2) non-atomic executing-guard on Coro.resume -- two threads read 'not executing' and both swap into one context (bug 76), plus uninit/reinit C-slot check-then-set; (3) CoLock non-atomic check-then-set acquire + current_g read blind on hubs (bug 93); (4) plain (non-atomic) accesses to shared scalars raced by concurrent readers/writers -- pump wake-fd double-create, select fired_case, preempt-hook pointer, sysmon resume_g (bug 115, C11 data races found only by TSan). The fix is ALWAYS a CAS/atomic or a genuinely lock-free structure, NEVER 'add a bigger flag'. Output each check-then-act site, the two racing operations, and the corrupted outcome (double-pop / double-swap / double-create / torn read).
grep_or_query: rg -nP 'if\s+[^=]*(empty|deque|not .*executing|not .*inited|flag|_state)\b[^=]*:\s*$' src/runloom/monkey src/runloom_c -- check-then-act in Python/C; plus plain (non-atomic) shared scalar access: rg -nP '\b(fired_case|resume_g|wake_fd|executing|_inited)\b' src/runloom_c | rg -v '__atomic|_Atomic|memory_order' (plain accesses to fields that are also written concurrently). clang-query: fields written on >1 thread without _Atomic. TSan under mn_stress is the reliable dynamic detector.
trigger_paths: src/runloom/monkey/{queues,events,locks}.py, src/runloom_c/coro.c, src/runloom_c/module_coro.c.inc, src/runloom_c/netpoll_pump.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_sysmon.c.inc; ANY new 'if <shared cond>: <mutate shared>' pattern, ANY new shared scalar field read/written from >1 thread, ANY new lazy 'if not created' guard on shared state.
variant_analysis: Given one instance: (a) grep every 'if <emptiness/flag/inited>: mutate' across monkey primitives and every plain shared-scalar field -- each is a candidate; (b) the fix is CAS/atomic/lock-free -- apply the same to sibling queue/lock/guard primitives (if CoSimpleQueue popleft was TOCTOU, audit every deque/list-backed queue and every executing/inited guard); (c) run the whole primitive set under TSan-FT-CPython + GIL-off high-thread-count fuzzing (these bugs still pass single-threaded tests, so only TSan/GIL-off exposes them); (d) confirm no 'bigger flag' band-aid was substituted for an actual atomic.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for weak-memory-fence-gap (runloom bugs 8,16,17,67). Dekker-shaped handshakes over TWO different memory locations written with release/acquire intuition are correct under sequential consistency but LOSE StoreLoad ordering under RC11 and even x86-TSO store buffering. Dev machines are x86 and SC Spin models VERIFY the buggy code, so only GenMC/herd7/CBMC and arm64 hardware exhibit the reorder. Audit every two-location handshake: (1) park_safe/wake_safe Dekker -- the store-my-flag / load-their-flag pair needs a seq_cst FENCE between store and load or the wake is lost (bug 16); (2) blockpool job.done Dekker, same shape, unmasked only after cache-line padding removed the accidental fix (bug 17); (3) Chase-Lev deque bottom/top handshake (bug 67); (4) stolen-g / struct publication -- the g POINTER must not become visible before its struct field stores complete, or a thief reads a phantom half-initialized g (arm64 connect storm, bug 8); the publishing store needs release AND the consuming load acquire, and on arm64 this is a real dmb, not free. For each handshake, state the two locations, the required fence, and whether the current code has it. Verify with GenMC/herd7 on the REAL extracted code, never an SC Spin model of a hand-transcription.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '__atomic_(store|load|exchange|compare_exchange)_n|memory_order_(relaxed|acquire|release|acq_rel|seq_cst)|atomic_thread_fence' src/runloom_c ; focus on Dekker shapes: a store to location A followed by a load of location B on the same thread without an intervening seq_cst fence -- clang-query for store-then-load of two distinct _Atomic globals in one function. Named seams: park_safe/wake_safe (mn_sched), cldeque.c (Chase-Lev), blockpool job.done, stolen-g publication (mn_sched_hub_main.c.inc).
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/mn_sched_hub_main.c.inc, src/runloom_c/mn_sched_hub_resume_preempt.c.inc, src/runloom_c/cldeque.c, src/runloom_c/plat_atomic.h, any new lock-free handshake or publish/consume of a struct-plus-its-pointer; ANY change to a memory_order argument or a two-location flag protocol.
variant_analysis: Given one fence gap: (a) enumerate every two-location handshake (`rg 'atomic_(store|load)'` grouped by function; find store-A-then-load-B pairs) -- park/wake, blockpool, Chase-Lev, steal-publication, and any new one; (b) extract each as the REAL TU/shim into a two-thread million-iteration executable and run on x86 AND arm64 (SB reordering is observable on x86-TSO itself, so the hammer exhibits the lost wake in minutes); (c) run GenMC/herd7 on the extracted code -- SC Spin is blind by construction; (d) objdump-assert seq_cst stores emit lock/xchg (x86) or dmb (arm64) so a shim (bug 67) that silently drops the fence is caught at build time; (e) stand up a nightly arm64 hardware lane for soak/lifefuzz.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for racy-lazy-init (runloom bugs 20,104,114). 'if not inited: create' on first use was safe under the GIL and becomes a CREATION STORM under free threading -- N racing first-callers each build a backend/fd/semaphore/socketpair and the losers' resources are used-then-orphaned. Flag EVERY lazy first-use init of a shared resource: (1) shared-epoll double-init arms sockets into ORPHANED epoll instances (loser's epoll is used then discarded) + a mask==0 ERR/HUP drop (bug 20); (2) lazy-init spawn semaphore raced so a cancel watcher captured an ORPHAN semaphore, and an OS-mutex guard blocked hub threads at 100k (bug 104); (3) context._wake_fd lazy-init created THOUSANDS of socketpairs under a cancellation storm on Windows -- fixed by eager import-time fd creation (bug 114). The fix is ALWAYS one of: eager single-threaded init BEFORE concurrency starts, or double-checked locking around the WHOLE bring-up (not just the pointer store) so losers tear down their partial resource. Output each lazy-init site, the shared resource it creates, and whether concurrent first-callers can orphan a copy.
grep_or_query: rg -nP 'if\s+(not\s+|!)?[A-Za-z_]*(inited|_init|created|is None|== NULL|_wake_fd|_epoll|_sem)\b.*:?\s*(\n|\{)?' src/runloom_c src/runloom | rg -iP 'init|create|socketpair|epoll_create|Semaphore|allocate' ; concretely rg -nP 'is None|== NULL|!.*inited' src/runloom/{context.py,monkey} src/runloom_c/netpoll_init.c.inc then check each is guarded by a once-CAS or double-checked lock around the WHOLE creation, not just the store. netpoll_init.c.inc already uses a once-init CAS -- use it as the reference pattern.
trigger_paths: src/runloom/context.py, src/runloom/monkey/{events,sockets,procutil}.py, src/runloom_c/netpoll_init.c.inc; ANY new lazy 'create on first use' of a shared fd/socketpair/semaphore/epoll/backend, ANY resource that could be created concurrently by racing first-callers.
variant_analysis: Given one racy-init: (a) grep every 'if not created/is None/!inited: create' of a SHARED (process- or module-global) resource; per-fiber/per-call resources are exempt; (b) the fix is eager-init-before-concurrency OR double-checked-locking-around-the-whole-bring-up -- apply whichever the resource allows to every sibling (wake_fd, spawn semaphore, epoll instance, socketpair); (c) confirm losers TEAR DOWN their partial resource rather than orphaning it (the orphan-into-an-unpumped-epoll / orphan-semaphore shape); (d) drive a cancellation/creation storm at high thread count (the bug 114 repro shape) to surface socketpair/fd exhaustion.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for fiber-stack-undersize (runloom bugs 12,112,113). Fibers hand CPython code a KB-scale C stack where every stdlib/library assumes 8MB; one fat frame or a deep C-call burst punches the guard page (297 distinct stdlib SIGSEGVs collapsed to this ONE cause). Check: (1) any single C frame that can exceed the fiber stack -- the select_select_impl reimpl had a 50.9KB frame that overflowed a 32KB fiber stack; such fat-frame code must be ROUTED OFF the fiber stack (offload to a pool) (bug 112); (2) code paths that C-recurse deeply or burst -- OpenSSL/kex warmup, asyncssh, aio protocol callbacks (data_received/connection_made) run user C-recursing code and must be PINNED to a larger stack (_go_io / _IO_STACK / _TASK_STACK = 512KB), never spawned on a bare small fiber (bug 113); (3) CPython's own SP-based recursion guard (c_stack_top/soft/hard, 3.14) must be RE-ARMED per fiber against the RIGHT (small) stack on every resume, with adequate margin -- armed on the wrong stack and never re-armed = silent overflow past a thin margin (bug 12). Note main-thread testing never runs on the small stack, so these are invisible without fiber execution. Output every fiber-executed code path, its worst-case C frame/recursion depth, and whether it is on an adequate stack with a correctly re-armed guard.
grep_or_query: rg -nP 'RUNLOOM_STACK|_IO_STACK|_TASK_STACK|_go_io|fiber\(|stack_size|c_stack_(top|soft|hard)|recursion' src/runloom_c src/runloom ; for fat-frame candidates: rg -nP 'char\s+\w+\[[0-9]{4,}\]|alloca|struct\s+\w+\s+\w+\[[0-9]{3,}\]' src/runloom_c src/runloom/monkey (large on-stack arrays); for callback-fibers on bare stacks: rg -nP 'data_received|connection_made|fiber\(' src/runloom/aio | rg -v '_go_io|_IO_STACK'.
trigger_paths: src/runloom/monkey/polling.py, src/runloom/aio/_base.py, src/runloom_c/coro.{c,h}, src/runloom_c/runloom_sched_core.c.inc (recursion re-arm); ANY new fiber-spawned code that runs user callbacks / C-extension code / crypto, ANY large on-stack buffer in fiber-reachable code, ANY CPython version bump touching the recursion-guard fields.
variant_analysis: Given one overflow: (a) grep every direct fiber( spawn and confirm callback/C-recursing paths use _go_io/_IO_STACK/_TASK_STACK not a bare small stack; (b) grep large on-stack arrays / alloca in fiber-reachable code and route fat frames to a pool; (c) confirm CPython's SP recursion guard is re-armed on the CORRECT stack on every resume (generated from the tstate privatization manifest); (d) run the stdlib corpus + asyncssh kex + OpenSSL warmup on the SMALL fiber stack (main-thread testing hides these) to surface guard-page SEGVs.

audit_prompt: You are auditing SUBSYSTEM for build-silent-elision (runloom bugs 67,84). The toolchain can compile CORRECTNESS OUT with zero diagnostics -- builds are clean, only behavior differs, and review reads the INTENDED code not the elided one. Check: (1) UAPI/feature #if gates -- a TU that #if-gates on IORING_* / EPOLL* / EV_* but is MISSING the defining header (<linux/io_uring.h>) silently selects the #else NO-OP STUB; the fix for a hang shipped as a stub reproducing the hang (bug 84). Verify every TU using an IORING_*/EPOLL*/EV_* gate actually INCLUDES the header that defines it, and that gates #if on an ALWAYS-DEFINED generated feature macro (RUNLOOM_HAVE_*), not a bare #ifdef that is false-by-absence. (2) atomic shims -- the MSVC __atomic_store_n shim dropped the memory order so a SEQ_CST store emitted a plain MOV, silently breaking the Chase-Lev Dekker (bug 67). Verify every atomic shim preserves ordering and that seq_cst stores actually emit the barrier instruction. Guard rails must be EXECUTABLE -- litmus tests, runtime feature self-checks, per-TU header asserts, objdump assertions -- because static review cannot see the elision. Output every feature gate and atomic shim, whether its enabling header/order is guaranteed present, and whether an executable canary confirms the emitted behavior.
grep_or_query: rg -nP '#if(def)?\s+.*\b(IORING_[A-Z_]+|EPOLL[A-Z]+|EV_[A-Z]+|AFD_POLL[A-Z_]*)\b' src/runloom_c -- for each hit TU, check `rg -L 'include.*io_uring.h|include.*epoll.h|include.*event.h'` on the SAME file (gate without its defining header = silent stub). Atomic shims: rg -nP '__atomic_(store|load|exchange).*n|_InterlockedExchange|MemoryBarrier' src/runloom_c/plat_atomic.h src/runloom_c/compat.h. Post-build: nm/objdump on the .so to diff emitted feature symbols against a committed per-platform matrix.
trigger_paths: src/runloom_c/plat_atomic.h, src/runloom_c/compat.h, src/runloom_c/plat.h, any .c/.inc adding an IORING_*/EPOLL*/EV_*/AFD_* #if gate, any change to the atomic shim or build feature detection; note the mn_sched TU is the known UAPI-macro-TU trap (memory project_pygo_uapi_macro_tu_trap).
variant_analysis: Given one silent elision: (a) generate runloom_features.h from one probe TU that includes EVERY UAPI header and emits RUNLOOM_HAVE_*=0/1; convert every bare #ifdef gate to #if RUNLOOM_HAVE_* (defined-or-error via -Werror=undef) so absence-of-header can no longer masquerade as feature-absence; (b) add a per-TU lint: any IORING_*/EPOLL*/EV_* token in a TU requires that TU to include the defining header; (c) add a startup self-check in test builds that fails on 'kernel supports it but this TU compiled the stub'; (d) for atomic shims, extract the Dekker seams into compiled two-thread hammers AND objdump-assert seq_cst stores emit lock/xchg (x86) / dmb (arm64); (e) post-build nm-diff emitted feature symbols against the committed per-platform matrix.
